research_profile.style_preference: 中英双语，中文在前，英文在后；克制、具体、有判断力，避免新闻稿式写作

topic_threads.theme: agentic training as closed-loop executable systems
topic_threads.summary: Recent papers are moving agent training away from static QA and toward executable environments: ClawGym uses local workspace tasks with code/rubric verifiers; FutureWorld uses real-world delayed outcomes and probabilistic rewards.

World2VLM treats a controllable world model as a source of action-conditioned transition supervision, suggesting a path where imagination is distilled into VLM parameters instead of invoked at every inference step.

DataPRM argues data agents need environment-aware process verifiers that can catch silent errors and avoid punishing recoverable exploration. EnterpriseDocBench shifts document AI evaluation from single answers to parsing-indexing-retrieval-generation pipelines and highlights answer completeness as a deployment risk.

open_questions: Can agent verifiers avoid becoming shortcuts that models learn to satisfy without completing the real task?
How should world-model teacher reliability be estimated per sample and per task before distillation?
For live prediction environments, how should delayed or unresolved outcomes be retained instead of discarded?
In document intelligence, how should answer completeness and auditability be reported alongside factual accuracy?

recent_digests.title: 从闭环训练到可审计数据智能
next_run_suggestions: Track public release and independent replication for ClawGym resources.
Search for reliability-weighted world-model distillation and multi-teacher agreement methods.
Search for data-agent PRMs tested on messy notebooks, spreadsheets, and enterprise file systems.
Follow document intelligence benchmarks that combine parsing, retrieval, generation, citation, and completeness evaluation.
